Telemarketing Script for User’s Choice
Where You Get Through to the Decision Maker
Receptionist: Hello, ____________ Construction. Can I help you? (If the receptionist
says his or her name, then repeat it after you say, “Hi” in the following response.)
BD Agent: Hi, my name is ________________, and I’m a state compliance advisor for a
registered training organization called (your companies name). Who’s the right person to
speak about employee training and development? Would that be the head of your HR
department or someone else?
Receptionist: What’s this in regards to?
BD Agent: It’s in regards to some state funding that’s become available to up-skill your
company’s workers. Who’s the right person to speak to about that? Is it the head of your
HR department or someone else?
Receptionist: That would be ________________, from HR.
BD Agent: Okay, great. Can I speak to him, please?
Receptionist: Sure, hold on a moment.
HR Person: Hello, this is ________________.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 1 of 4 --

BD Agent: Hi, _______________. My name is _____________ and I’m a state
compliance advisor for a registered training organization called (your companies name). I
was told that you’re the (wo)man to speak to when it comes to training and development
for your company’s employees. Is that the case, mate?
HR Person: Yes, it is.
BD Agent: Okay, great! Well, ___________, the reason for the call today is that the state
has just started phasing out the old WH&S system, in favor of nationally recognized trade
qualifications … and they’re allowing us to provide 100% funding to companies like
yours, in the _______________ industry, so you can get your workers their new trade
qualifications without having to take a dime out of pocket.
Now, roughly, mate, how many people do you have out in the field right now … just a
ballpark?
HR Person: About ____ people, more or less.
BD Agent: Okay, perfect, mate. Well, what I’d like to do right now, with your
permission, ____________, is email you a bit of information on my company (your
companies name), along with a quick explanation of how the government funding works,
and then get back to you in the next few days or so, so we discuss things in more detail.
Sound good?
HR-person: Yeah, sure, mate.
BD Agent: Okay, great! Now, let me ask you just a couple of quick questions, so I can
tailor the email to directly suits your needs. First of all:
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 2 of 4 --

1) 	Of the roughly _______ people you have out in the field right now, what sorts of
jobs are they doing day-to-day? Any road construction … pipe laying …
commercial driving … or anything else like that?
2) 	And what type of “kit” do you currently have out there on site?
3) 	And in terms of the actual employment status of your guys, can you give me a
quick breakdown? How many of them are casual or permanent part time, versus
permanent full time.
4) 	And do they work under an EBA?
5) 	Okay, great! And, mate what’s your email address, mate, so I can get this info out
to you right now?
6) 	And the address I have on file for your company is ____________. Is that correct,
mate?
BD Agent: Okay great! You know, I’m actually going to be up in your area next week, on
_________ and __________, and I’d be more than happy to stop by to discuss things in
more detail. Which day is better for you, mate, ________ or _________.
HR Person: __________ works.
BD Agent: Okay, perfect. And what time is better for you, morning or afternoon? I’m
flexile either way.
HR Person: _____________ is better.
BD Agent: Okay, great! How about _______ o’clock? Does that work?
HR Person: Yes.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 3 of 4 --

BD Agent: Okay, great. I’ll put that in my calendar right now. In the meantime, I’ll put
my cellphone number in the email, so if you have any questions at all you can give me a
shout. Sound good?
HR Person: Yup!
BD Agent: Okay, great. I’ll get that email out to you straight away. And I look forward to
seeing you next week. Have a great day!
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 4 of 4 --
